problem_description:
HTML entity parser is the parser that takes HTML code as input and replace all the entities of the special characters by the characters itself.
The special characters and their entities for HTML are:

Quotation Mark: the entity is " and symbol character is ".
Single Quote Mark: the entity is ' and symbol character is '.
Ampersand: the entity is & and symbol character is &.
Greater Than Sign: the entity is > and symbol character is >.
Less Than Sign: the entity is < and symbol character is <.
Slash: the entity is ⁄ and symbol character is /.

Given the input text string to the HTML parser, you have to implement the entity parser.
Return the text after replacing the entities by the special characters.
 
Example 1:

Input: text = "& is an HTML entity but &ambassador; is not."
Output: "& is an HTML entity but &ambassador; is not."
Explanation: The parser will replace the & entity by &

Example 2:

Input: text = "and I quote: "...""
Output: "and I quote: \"...\""

 
Constraints:

1 <= text.length <= 105
The string may contain any possible characters out of all the 256 ASCII characters.
starter_code:
class Solution:
    def entityParser(self, text: str) -> str:
test:
def check(candidate):
    assert candidate(text = "& is an HTML entity but &ambassador; is not.") == "& is an HTML entity but &ambassador; is not."
    assert candidate(text = "and I quote: "..."") == "and I quote: \"...\""
    assert candidate(text = "Stay home! Practice on Leetcode :)") == "Stay home! Practice on Leetcode :)"
    assert candidate(text = "x > y && x < y is always False") == "x > y && x < y is always False"
    assert candidate(text = "leetcode.com⁄problemset⁄all") == "leetcode.com/problemset/all"

input_output.input: text = "& is an HTML entity but &ambassador; is not."
input_output.output: "& is an HTML entity but &ambassador; is not."

input_output.input: text = "x > y && x < y is always false"
input_output.output: "x > y && x < y is always false"

input_output.input: text = "He said, "'Hello, & welcome to the world of >HTML<!'""
input_output.output: He said, "'Hello, & welcome to the world of >HTML<!'"

input_output.input: text = "This ⁄example⁄ should have slashes replaced everywhere.⁄⁄⁄"
input_output.output: This /example/ should have slashes replaced everywhere.///

input_output.input: text = "&quot; and &apos; should be " and ' respectively"
text = ">&gt;&gt; Greater than signs <&lt;&lt; and &amp;>&amp;< mixed up <&lt;&lt; >&gt;&gt;"
input_output.output: >>> Greater than signs <<< and &>&< mixed up <<< >>>

input_output.input: text = "&&&& is not a valid HTML entity, but &amp;&amp; is "&&""
input_output.output: &&&& is not a valid HTML entity, but && is "&&"

query:
You are an expert Python programmer. You will be given a question (problem specification) and will generate a correct Python program that matches the specification and passes all tests.

### Question:
HTML entity parser is the parser that takes HTML code as input and replace all the entities of the special characters by the characters itself.
The special characters and their entities for HTML are:

Quotation Mark: the entity is " and symbol character is ".
Single Quote Mark: the entity is ' and symbol character is '.
Ampersand: the entity is & and symbol character is &.
Greater Than Sign: the entity is > and symbol character is >.
Less Than Sign: the entity is < and symbol character is <.
Slash: the entity is ⁄ and symbol character is /.

Given the input text string to the HTML parser, you have to implement the entity parser.
Return the text after replacing the entities by the special characters.
 
Example 1:

Input: text = "& is an HTML entity but &ambassador; is not."
Output: "& is an HTML entity but &ambassador; is not."
Explanation: The parser will replace the & entity by &

Example 2:

Input: text = "and I quote: "...""
Output: "and I quote: \"...\""

 
Constraints:

1 <= text.length <= 105
The string may contain any possible characters out of all the 256 ASCII characters.



### Format: You will use the following starter code to write the solution to the problem and enclose your code within delimiters.
```python
class Solution:
    def entityParser(self, text: str) -> str:
```

### Answer: (use the provided format with backticks)
response:
```python
class Solution:
    def entityParser(self, text: str) -> str:
        # Define a dictionary to map HTML entities to their corresponding characters
        html_entities = {
            """: '"',
            "'": "'",
            "&": "&",
            ">": ">",
            "<": "<",
            "⁄": "/"
        }
        
        # Iterate over the dictionary and replace each entity in the text
        for entity, char in html_entities.items():
            text = text.replace(entity, char)
        
        return text
```

This solution defines a dictionary that maps each HTML entity to its corresponding special character. It then iterates over this dictionary, replacing each entity found in the input text with the corresponding character. The `str.replace()` method is used for this purpose. The function returns the modified text after all replacements have been made.
